title: Houses for Rent in Cleveland Under $1,100
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Cleveland Under $1,100/mo
metaDescription: Browse houses for rent in Cleveland under $1,100 a month. See what fits by bedroom size, where inventory is, and Section 8-friendly homes. Book a showing.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent cleveland ohio under 1100
answerBox: Under $1,100 a month is a comfortable budget for most 2-bedroom houses in Cleveland and opens up some 3-bedroom homes as well. Citywide rents in our available homes run about $700 to $1,800, with a typical rent near $1,000 — meaning $1,100 sits right around the middle of the local market.

body: A $1,100 budget covers the majority of 2-bedroom houses and duplexes in Cleveland and reaches into the lower end of the 3-bedroom market too. Our own Cleveland selection has a typical (median) rent near $1,000, so $1,100 sits close to the citywide middle — enough to open real options without competing for the most expensive listings.

We work with rental homes across Cleveland, concentrated on the east and southeast side, and every one accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) and tell us $1,100 is your ceiling — we'll show you what's realistically available now.

sections.h2: Rent by bedroom size around the $1,100 mark
body: Zumper's July 2026 report put Cleveland's citywide median rent at $1,250 overall and $1,100 for 2-bedroom units specifically — meaning $1,100 lines up almost exactly with the market-wide 2-bedroom median. Our currently-available 2-bedroom inventory ranges $750–$1,100 (average around $960), and 3-bedroom inventory starts around $1,100 at the low end, so this budget captures both sizes at their entry points.

Our inventory is concentrated in east and southeast Cleveland — **Slavic Village (44105)**, **Collinwood (44110)**, **Glenville (44108)**, **Fairfax/Central (44104)**, **Hough (44103)**, and **Buckeye-Shaker (44120)** — where a mix of early-1900s single-family homes, duplexes, triplexes, and fourplexes make up the rental stock. We also manage a smaller number of west-side homes (Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway, Old Brooklyn) and homes in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria, Ohio.

items: **East/Southeast Cleveland:** widest selection at this price point
**West side (Cudell, Old Brooklyn):** smaller but present inventory
**Outside Cleveland proper:** Akron, Lorain, Elyria — occasional homes

sections.h2: Comparing nearby price bands and sizes
body: If a 3-bedroom home with more room is the priority, our [under-$1,200](/cleveland-rentals/houses/houses-for-rent-cleveland-ohio-under-1200/) and [under-$1,500](/cleveland-rentals/houses/houses-for-rent-cleveland-ohio-under-1500/) pages open up more of that inventory. For a strictly 2-bedroom search, see our [2-bedroom houses page](/cleveland-rentals/houses/2-bedroom-houses-for-rent-cleveland-ohio/), and for households needing more space without going above this budget, our [duplex page](/cleveland-rentals/houses/duplex-for-rent-cleveland-ohio/) explains how duplex living compares to a standalone house.

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers in this price range
body: Every home we work with accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, at any rent level in our available homes. If you're using a voucher, your actual rent responsibility depends on your household's income and the current CMHA payment standard for your bedroom size — verify the current figures directly at [cmha.net](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) before settling on a target rent.

faq.q: What kind of house can I rent in Cleveland for under $1,100?
faq.a: A $1,100 budget comfortably covers most 2-bedroom houses and duplexes and reaches the lower end of the 3-bedroom market in Cleveland. It lines up closely with Zumper's July 2026 citywide 2-bedroom median of $1,100, making it a realistic, middle-of-the-market target.

faq.q: Can I get a 3-bedroom house for under $1,100 in Cleveland?
faq.a: It's possible but not guaranteed — our currently-available 3-bedroom inventory starts around $1,100 at the low end, so a home at that price point exists but competes with a smaller pool of listings. Book a showing to check what's currently open in that range.

faq.q: Where in Cleveland has the most houses under $1,100?
faq.a: East and southeast Cleveland neighborhoods — Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax/Central, Hough, and Buckeye-Shaker — carry the largest share of our inventory in this price range, with a smaller number of homes on the west side and in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria.

faq.q: Do houses in this price range accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Yes. Every home we work with, at any rent level including under $1,100, accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Confirm your voucher's current payment standard with CMHA to know your actual budget.
